hook.text: A dev used prod credentials for testing. A bug deleted real customer data. Isolation prevents this.

buildup.text: Dev, staging, and prod need separate credentials, databases, and access. Never share them.

discovery.text: Each environment gets its own secrets in the manager. Developers can't even view production secrets.

twist.text: Network isolation adds another layer. Prod runs in its own VPC. Dev machines can't reach it directly.

climax.text: Terraform workspaces and K8s namespaces make isolation repeatable. Spin up separate envs easily.

punchline.text: Separate environments fully. What you can't reach, you can't break.

quiz.question: Why should development and production use separate credentials?
options: To prevent developer mistakes from affecting real customer data
